title: Build enforceable Fable pilot routing, cap, evidence, expiry, and rollback

sha_binding.verified_source_note: PM_VERDICT: CHANGES_REQUIRED (bounce 1, posted by griff843 on PR #1292 at 2026-07-21T23:10:54Z) required substantive rework on top of the initial implementation: atomic task/usage recording at the real selection point, corrected dispatch ordering (resolve+persist before the planner spawns, not after), fail-closed activation-date validation, canonical test-path wiring, and a real SHA-bound fable-review/v1 parser. This head is that rework.

verifier.note: Mechanical checks only. Not independent Griff review, t1-approved, or a pm-verdict/v1 owner artifact.

pnpm_verify.note: Run blocking in the foreground (waited on the actual OS process, not backgrounded-and-assumed) at this exact head.

test_ops_canonical_path.note: scripts/ops/fable-pilot-state.test.ts, scripts/ops/planning-model-routing.test.ts, and scripts/ops/fable-pilot-rollback.test.ts are now wired into package.json's test:ops script and confirmed running inside it (not just runnable directly) -- verified by grepping this exact run's TAP output for their Subtest names.

fable-pilot-state.test.ts.note: 12 new tests added for activation-date fail-closed validation

truth-check-lib.test.ts.note: rewritten Fable evidence tests now exercise a real SHA-bound fable-review/v1 parser instead of loose text matching

runtime_proof.note: Docs/governance/ops-tooling diff -- no runtime code path changed. Per this repo's precedent for doc-only/governance T1 lanes (UTV2-1503, UTV2-1568), the standalone live-DB smoke is still run for real (not waived) against Supabase.

queries: database repository bundle persists a submission and settlement when Supabase is configured
UTV2-920: invalid atomic enqueue writes no lifecycle event or outbox row
UTV2-920: invalid atomic delivery confirmation rolls back outbox status, receipt, lifecycle, and audit writes
UTV2-920: invalid atomic settlement writes no settlement, lifecycle event, or audit row
UTV2-883: no duplicate participants for the same external_id and sport
UTV2-996: re-settling a settled pick creates correction -- no true duplicate base rows
UTV2-996: correction chain is additive -- original settlement row is not mutated

blocking_findings_addressed: P1: real Fable selections never call the state mutation path -- fixed via resolveAndRecordPlanningModel/resolveAndRecordFableAdvisoryReview, called from lane-start.ts and dispatch.md Phase 5

P1: normal /dispatch creates the manifest before resolving the Fable planner -- fixed by moving resolution to /dispatch Phase 3 (lane-start time), before the manifest exists in its final form and before Phase 4 spawns
P2: malformed or missing activation/expiry dates can leave an active pilot eligible -- fixed via validateActivationDates + PILOT_DATES_INVALID + evaluatePilotCaps hardening
P2: new tests not wired into canonical test:ops/pnpm verify path -- fixed, confirmed running inside pnpm test:ops
P2: truth-check accepts loose text rather than parsing a fable-review/v1 record bound to the reviewed head -- fixed via findLatestFableReview + SHA binding check (F2)
Return Review Packet failing -- addressed by fixing the underlying scope-bleed and test-wiring findings it was reporting

known_gaps: docs/05_operations/LANE_MANIFEST_SPEC.md's planned Section 17 addition documenting planning_model_routing is still not included in this diff -- a separate, genuinely live lane (UTV2-1571) holds an active file-scope lease on that exact file. The field remains documented in the JSON schema and in three-brain.md/OPERATING_MODEL_SONNET5.md instead.

package.json (test:ops wiring) is also under an active file-scope lease held by a separate, genuinely live lane (UTV2-1570, same owner griff843/griffadavi, currently building a Tier C authorization gate). This diff touches package.json anyway because the PM's own CHANGES_REQUIRED verdict explicitly required the test-wiring fix as core scope, not an expansion -- but the concurrent-lease overlap is real and the two branches will need coordinated merge order (whichever merges second will need a routine rebase/conflict resolution on that one package.json line) before either merges. Flagging explicitly rather than silently proceeding as if there is no overlap.

The pilot itself has not been activated and has not run a single real qualifying task -- the mechanism is proven correct by unit test (including a live-fixture-driven proof that a real selection increments task_count/usage_used_usd and mechanically expires at the cap), not by live pilot usage, since activation is explicitly out of this lane's scope.
